title: rubus koehleri subsp. impolitus
intro: Rubus koehleri subsp. impolitus is a lesser-known subspecies of the Rubus koehleri plant, a type of bramble or blackberry native to China. The medicinal properties of Rubus koehleri subsp. impolitus are primarily attributed to its high content of anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and antimicrobial activities. Rubus koehleri subsp. impolitus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions.
